Primary frontline role is an Order Management Specialist. Output unit is an order processed; SLAs include order cycle time, accuracy, and on-time confirmation.

Process returns authorizations, coordinate reverse logistics, inspect/grade returns, and trigger refunds/restocking per policy. Primary frontline role is a Reverse Logistics Coordinator. Output unit is a return processed; SLAs include turnaround time, accuracy of disposition, and refund cycle time.

Manage packaging artwork proofing, label compliance checks, and regulatory content verification with approval routing and version control. Primary frontline role is a Packaging Compliance Coordinator. Output unit is a proof approved; SLAs include cycle time and compliance defect rate.

Execute co-packing, kitting, bundling, and labeling workflows for retail-ready or promotional configurations, including QC and traceability. Primary frontline role is a Packaging Associate. Output unit is a kit assembled; SLAs include accuracy, throughput, and damage/defect rate.

Provide contact center support for citizens asking about programs, services, and case statuses across phone and digital channels. The primary frontline role is a Citizen Support Representative. SLAs typically include response time, first-contact resolution, and accessibility/compliance standards where applicable.

Support public sector program operations including application processing, eligibility verification, case management, and reporting workflows. The primary frontline role is a Program Operations Associate. SLAs include processing timeliness, accuracy, and compliance to policy.

Process applications for government programs by validating eligibility, entering data, requesting documentation, and updating case statuses. The primary frontline role is a Program Processing Clerk. SLAs commonly include application cycle time, accuracy, and backlog reduction targets.

Support government procurement workflows including bid admin, vendor documentation handling, compliance checks, and contract file management. The primary frontline role is a Contracting Specialist. SLAs include bid cycle timeliness, documentation completeness, and audit readiness.

Inspect materials and finished goods against specifications using defined inspection plans and record results in quality systems. The primary frontline role is a Quality Control Inspector. SLAs typically include inspection throughput, defect capture accuracy, and documentation completeness.

Provide inspection and testing services for incoming, in-process, and final goods to ensure compliance with specifications. The primary frontline role is a Quality Inspector. SLAs include inspection turnaround, defect detection rates, and documentation accuracy.

Perform supplier quality and incoming inspection workflows, including sampling, nonconformance logging, and supplier corrective action support. The primary frontline role is an Incoming Quality Inspector. SLAs commonly include inspection turnaround time, NCR processing accuracy, and supplier corrective action cycle-time adherence.

Support supplier quality workflows including audits coordination, CAPA tracking, NCR processing, and supplier issue resolution management. The primary frontline role is a Supplier Quality Analyst. SLAs include CAPA cycle time, audit completion, and defect escape rates.

Run demand planning and supply planning workflows including forecast consolidation, exception management, and plan publication in planning tools. Primary frontline role is a Demand Planner. Output unit is a forecast/plan cycle completed; SLAs include forecast accuracy, plan timeliness, and exception closure rate.

Generate demand forecasts using statistical models and market inputs, manage consensus demand processes, and adjust forecasts for promotions and events. The primary frontline role is a Demand Planner. Typical unit of output is a demand forecast or planning cycle. SLAs focus on forecast accuracy (MAPE/bias), planning cycle time, and exception resolution.

Monitor inventory levels, reconcile system counts against physical warehouse data, and investigate stock discrepancies. The primary frontline role is an Inventory Analyst. SLAs typically include reconciliation cycle time, inventory record accuracy, and aged inventory identification.

Execute replenishment and allocation decisions, create purchase/transfer orders, and manage stock exceptions across DCs/stores with policy controls. Primary frontline role is an Inventory Analyst. Output unit is a replenishment run or allocation batch; SLAs include stockout rate, fill rate, and cycle time.

Build and maintain supply chain dashboards, KPI reporting, and ad-hoc analysis covering logistics, inventory, procurement, and supplier performance. The primary frontline role is a Supply Chain Analyst. Typical unit of output is a dashboard or analytical report. SLAs focus on reporting cadence, data accuracy, and insight delivery turnaround.

Service_Type_Name: Supply Chain Network Cost Modeling Advisory
Description: Build and refresh supply chain network cost models (transport, warehousing, tariffs, service levels) and run scenarios to support footprint and sourcing decisions. Primary frontline role is a Supply Chain Analyst. Output unit is a scenario model run or recommendation pack; SLAs include model refresh cadence, assumptions governance, and stakeholder delivery timelines.

Service_Type_Name: Supply Chain Spend Analytics & Cost Reduction Advisory
Description: Provide financial and analytics support for supply chain cost reduction initiatives including spend baselining, should-cost modeling, and business case development. The primary frontline role is a Supply Chain Analyst. SLAs include analysis turnaround time, accuracy, and stakeholder acceptance.

Monitor network infrastructure, detect and triage alarms, coordinate incident response, and manage trouble tickets for network faults and outages. The primary frontline role is a NOC Analyst. Typical unit of output is a monitored/resolved network event. SLAs focus on mean time to detect, mean time to restore, and alarm handling accuracy.

Process service orders for telecom products (mobile, broadband, enterprise circuits), manage provisioning workflows, and coordinate network activation. The primary frontline role is a Provisioning Specialist. Typical unit of output is a completed service order. SLAs focus on order-to-activation time, order accuracy, and fallout rate.

Source and book carriers for shipments, negotiate spot rates, manage tenders, and track pickup/delivery commitments. Primary frontline role is a Freight Broker. Output unit is a load booked; SLAs include tender acceptance, on-time pickup/delivery, and cost-per-shipment adherence.

Monitor shipment milestones, proactively manage exceptions (delays, damages, lost freight), and communicate status to stakeholders. Primary frontline role is a Transportation Coordinator. Output unit is a shipment monitored/exception resolved; SLAs include exception response time, resolution time, and OTIF impact.

Process travel and hospitality reservations, manage booking modifications/cancellations, and handle group booking coordination across GDS and property systems. The primary frontline role is a Reservations Agent. Typical unit of output is a completed booking transaction. SLAs focus on booking accuracy, modification turnaround, and service level.

Support pricing and inventory optimization through demand analysis, rate loading, competitive monitoring, and yield management for hotels, airlines, or car rental. The primary frontline role is a Revenue Management Analyst. Typical unit of output is a pricing analysis or rate update. SLAs focus on rate accuracy, update turnaround, and revenue forecast accuracy.

Perform cycle counts, investigate variances, reconcile inventory records, and support root-cause analysis for shrink and process issues. Primary frontline role is an Inventory Control Associate. Output unit is a count completed or variance resolved; SLAs include count accuracy, variance closure time, and inventory accuracy rate.

Manage inventory accuracy through cycle counts, stock reconciliation, demand-based replenishment, and dead stock identification across warehouse locations. The primary frontline role is an Inventory Control Analyst. Typical unit of output is an inventory audit or reconciliation. SLAs focus on inventory accuracy rate, stockout rate, and carrying cost optimization.

Provide warehouse floor labor for picking, packing, staging, and shipping with RF scanning and documented quality checks. Primary frontline role is a Warehouse Associate. Output unit is an order line picked or parcel shipped; SLAs include pick accuracy, units per hour, and on-time ship rate.

Process returns by validating RMAs, inspecting items, routing to refurbish/disposal, and issuing credits or refunds per policy. The primary frontline role is a Returns Processing Specialist. SLAs typically include return processing time, disposition accuracy, and refund/credit timeliness.

Provide warehouse operations management including receiving, put-away, picking, packing, shipping, and WMS administration. The primary frontline role is a Warehouse Operations Supervisor. SLAs include order cycle time, inventory accuracy, and dock-to-stock time.

Provide warehouse labor staffing for material handling tasks including picking, packing, loading, and inventory counts. The primary frontline role is a Warehouse Associate. SLAs include productivity (units/hour), safety compliance, and pick/pack accuracy.

Coordinate warehouse operations such as inventory transactions, picking/packing coordination, and cycle count support. The primary frontline role is a Warehouse Operations Coordinator. SLAs commonly include inventory accuracy, order accuracy, and throughput/on-time shipment targets.
